narrative.summary: The 'insanely fun soju spot' per Bon Appétit's March 2026 NYC food guide. Korean-inspired cocktails, soju-forward. 4.8★ (85 reviews). On the Bushwick→Ridgewood walk.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Cool atmosphere. Fun drinks. Really nice low to the ground tables that I personally fancied. We got the fried ribs, the tofu, and the kimchi carbonara. Everything was hella delicious. Beautiful textures and spice profiles. Great place to share plates with friends. Super fast service"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "1 The fries are super good. 2 All the ferments and pickles in the banchan are professionally made - the cranberry kimchi is bonkers and wholly unique. 3 The cocktails are good even if the texture of makgeoli is new to you. 4 10/10 music tn"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Such a cool experience. We really enjoyed the delicious and inventive cocktails, as well as the truly memorable (and substantial) anju/bar snacks. Our favorites were the kimchi carbonara and the gochujang fried ribs, but the dubu nuggets were a surprise hit. Don't sleep on the Space Spam cocktail! What a unique treasure in Bushwick!"
